landing.heroSubtitle: Describe your product, get a forensic audit of UX, payments, security, and more. In minutes.
landing.howSub: From description to actionable report in three steps.
landing.step1Body: Explain your stack, features, and target users.
landing.step3Body: A prioritised list of issues with fix suggestions.
landing.sampleTitle: Here's what a real report looks like
landing.sampleSub: Every audit ships with a prioritised list of issues and concrete fixes.
landing.sampleSummary: Solid foundations with friction points in checkout and onboarding.
landing.finalSub: Catch the bugs, friction, and security holes before your users ever see them.

sampleIssues.i1Title: Failed Stripe payments leave users on a blank screen
sampleIssues.i1Detail: When a card is declined, the spinner disappears and the page reverts to the cart with no error state. Users retry blindly or abandon.
sampleIssues.i1Fix: Catch the Stripe error from confirmCardPayment and surface a contextual message with retry and switch-method CTAs.
sampleIssues.i2Title: Anon key used for privileged writes
sampleIssues.i2Detail: The /invite endpoint writes to the members table from the browser without an RLS policy. Anyone can add themselves to any pot.
sampleIssues.i2Fix: Move the insert behind a server function and add an RLS policy: insert allowed only when auth.uid() = inviter_id.
sampleIssues.i3Title: First-run empty state has no guidance
sampleIssues.i3Detail: After signup, users land on an empty dashboard with no explanation of what a 'pot' is or how to create one.
sampleIssues.i3Fix: Add an empty-state card with a one-line explanation, an example screenshot, and a 'Create your first pot' button.

audit.title: What are we testing today?
audit.sub: The more detail you give, the sharper the audit.
audit.descPh: e.g. A React/TypeScript web app with Stripe payments targeting French students. Key features: shared payment pots, member invitations, SEPA transfers, Apple Pay. Currently in pre-launch with 0 users.
audit.descHelp: Be specific — describe your tech stack, key features, payment flows, and target users. The more detail you give, the more accurate your audit.
audit.instructionPh: e.g. Pay extra attention to the checkout flow
audit.addMoreDetail: Please add more detail for an accurate audit (minimum 200 characters).
audit.noCredits: You have no credits left. Buy more to continue.
audit.failed: Audit failed — see error details below.
audit.thinkingNote: Thinking like 8 expert testers. This usually takes 30–60 seconds.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.sub: Everything you need to know about Audit.ai.

items.a: Audit.ai is an AI-powered product analysis tool. It generates structured quality reports from your product description — it is not a substitute for real QA testing.
Describe your product, pick the categories you want analyzed, and get a detailed report in minutes.
No, credits are non-refundable once purchased. But each audit costs as little as €1.25, depending on your plan.
Reports are based on AI reasoning, not live testing. The more detail you provide in your description, the sharper and more relevant the results.
Founders, indie developers, and product managers who want a fast pre-launch sanity check on their product.
Your descriptions are sent to Anthropic for processing and are not stored beyond your generated report.

items.q: What if the audit times out?
items.a: No credit is deducted on failed audits. You can simply try again.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.p1: Audit.ai provides AI-generated product quality analysis reports based on user-submitted descriptions. Reports are not a substitute for professional QA testing.
terms.p2: Credits are non-refundable once purchased. One credit equals one audit report.
terms.p3: Audit.ai makes no guarantee of the accuracy or completeness of reports. All reports are AI-generated and should be reviewed by the user before being acted upon.
terms.p4: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts in cases of abuse, fraudulent activity, or violation of these terms.
terms.p5: These terms are governed by French law. Audit.ai is operated by Fortis Invest, Paris, France. Any disputes shall be subject to the jurisdiction of the courts of Paris.
terms.p6Pre: For any questions about these terms, contact

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.l1b: Password (stored as a salted hash, never in plain text)
privacy.l1c: Audit submissions (the product descriptions you provide)
privacy.l1d: Payment data (processed by Stripe, not stored by us)
privacy.p2: We share the minimum necessary data with the following sub-processors:
privacy.l2c: Anthropic — AI processing of your audit descriptions
privacy.p3Pre: Under the RGPD you have the right to access, correct, or delete your data at any time. To exercise these rights, contact
privacy.p4: We do not use any cookies beyond what is strictly necessary for session authentication. No tracking or advertising cookies are set.
privacy.p5: Audit reports are retained for 12 months from creation, after which they are automatically deleted. Account data is retained until you request deletion.
